data.content_ref: inline:{"claim":"An overproduction of elite warrior class coupled with ideological erosion eroded legitimacy, causing systemic sociopolitical collapse.","mechanism":"Systemic sociopolitical instability via internal elite competition, loss of cultic authority, and failure of succession mechanisms.","scope":{"covers":["Mycenaean Greece","Hittite Anatolia","Ugarit","Levantine city‑states"],"excludes":["Egypt"]},"forbidden":[{"case":"Evidence of continued stable royal succession in Mycenaean palaces until 1100 BCE.","eval":"rubric:std-hist"}],"prose_notes":"Critical historiographical essay interrogating elite recruitment patterns and religious texts."}
